title: [Feature]: Allow all transports for image pull in RestApi/libpod
body:
### Feature request description

Currently if the RestApi/libpod is used there is a check which only allows to pull images via the docker transport (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/api/handlers/libpod/images_pull.go#L54-L58).
This is also documented in the method description (_"Other transports are rejected as they do not make sense in a remote context."_). In general I agree but in my use case it would be good to be not limited by this.

**My use case is:**
I have podman running on an embedded system and a other process checks if a usb stick is plugged into the system. If so it checks if there are new containers on the usb stick and then instruct podman (via RestApi) to pull the containers via the oci transport.

### Suggest potential solution

The simplest solution is to just remove the check (I did this and tested it with the `oci` transport, which just worked).
Maybe a better solution would be to check if it is a local connection and only allow non docker transports than.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

It would also be possible execute (`os.Exec()`) the `podman pull oci:/...` command directly but this would be a mix of `libpod` and direct calls.

### Additional context

I think other locally running tools (like `podman-tui`) which uses the RestApi should be able to use all transports during a pull.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out and sharing your use case, @mwopfner!

The problem with the non-docker transports is a certain ambiguity of their sources.  Does a `podman-remote pull oci:/tmp/foo` relate to `/tmp/foo` on the client or on the host?

Your solution of running it directly on the device is also what I'd recommend.  Maybe automate it with a systemd service that checks for new images on a regular base?

@vrothberg I didn't thought about the problem with client and host for `podman-remote`. So now I at least understand why it is done :wink: 
But I if it would only be allowed for local connections (`localhost`) this problem would not exist (at least if we use absolute path) and tools like `podman-tui` could use it.

@mwopfner I sympathize with the idea but I fear it's too restricted to open up the possibility.  The most robust way is to exec `/usr/bin/podman`.  An alternative in Go would be to use `https://github.com/containers/common/tree/main/libimage` as Podman does internally.  We do our best to keep the API of `libimage` stable but there's no guarantee at thepresent.

I can see the arguments (#11593 is related) but it is really unfortunately because half of the use cases (commands run on the remote) would work but are blocked by the check. But I also understand that the interface is not clear for the user in this case, so disabling it completely makes also sense.

- The only solution to this would be an extra argument which specifies if the path is meant for `client` or `remote` and if a transport is not usable in one context and error could be returned. 
This is a check that `podman-remote` on the client should perform an probably nothing the `RestApi` on the `remote` must check. But to be compatible with older `podman-remote` versions I guess there should still be a check on the `remote` and an extra parameter which enables the other transports in the context of the `remote`.

- I think using `/usr/bin/podman pull` for now is the best option for me to get it working.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I don't see this as easily being solved. and I am not sure how it work in remote mode with the files on the client side.

@rhatdan: I thing your correct. Getting this working properly (so that the user also know how to use it correctly) is not easy. I guess technically the most functions should be available to implement it (`podman scp` already allows to copy images from local to remote) but getting the cli interface correct is tedious.
An alternative that may be _much_ easier to implement is to support non-archive inputs for `podman-remote load`.   Would that work for you, @mwopfner ?

@vrothberg What I want to achieve is to load an oci archive which is present on the remote (plugged in usb stick) via the rest API (see: UseCase in the first post). So I do not think extending `podman-remote load` will help me here.
Thanks for the reminder.  For a moment, I forgot about the server-side requirement.

No problem :wink:. I have a workaround, so this is not blocking me, but it would be nice to have a proper solution for this ... :thinking: 

Actually I think, maybe I could use `podman-remote load` in my case, because I'm using the Rest API from a process running on the remote, so I can also access the usb stick from this "remote" process. 
It is probably not the most efficient solution, but it should work.

After reading the `podman-remote load` docs and testing it it turns out that it does not work, because I use `oci-dir` which is currently not supported. But seeing `podman-remote load` got me thinking that the only thing (except for remote support for `oci-dir` and `docker-dir`) missing for `podman --remote pull` is to specify the context in which the pull should operate.
Maybe a `--context=local`, `--context=remote` could be enough.

`podman --remote pull --context=local oci-archive:~/alpine.tar` pulls it from the oci tar in my home folder on this machine
`podman --remote pull --context=remote oci:/mnt/usb-stick/alpine` pulls it from the oci folder `/mnt/usb-stick/alpine` on the remote

During testing the API I also run `podman --remote pull skopeo` which showed:
```
Resolved "skopeo" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull quay.io/skopeo/stable:latest...
```
but I could not find any explanation in the docs if `000-shortnames.conf` is used from my pc or the remote one.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@vrothberg what should we do with this one?
I do not think Podman should support loading archives on the server-side.  This would somehow break (a bit) the client/server model of podman-remote.
@mwopfner Are you interested in working on this?  @vrothberg Do you think we should close?
As mentioned above, I don't think remote should support it.
